Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, observe the overall process: All cases start with OR, then MS, QS. Then AS appears, but there are variations: some cases have IQC and multiple AS (like rework loop), some have TST and possibly RT (retest loop), some skip IQC entirely, some skip LB.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: AS, then possibly IQC, then AS again. In case 3: AS, IQC, AS, IQC, AS (so two IQC between AS). So its a loop: execute AS, then may execute IQC and then repeat AS.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: loop * (AS, IQC) works. after AS, you can either exit (go to TST) or do IQC and then AS again.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: after the AS loop, we have TST. Then possibly RT and then TST again (retest loop). In cases 1,3,5 we see TST, then RT, then TST again; in case 5 even multiple RT-TST cycles.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: another loop: * (TST, RT). After TST, either exit or do RT and then TST again.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then after TST loop, we have PK, then LB, then DP, D. But LB is optional (skipped in case 2).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: choice: do LB or skip it. So either LB or silent transition. after PK, there is a choice between LB and skip, but both lead to DP then D.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The entire process is sequential: OR->MS->QS->(AS loop)->(TST loop)->PK->(choice LB/skip)->DP->D. No parallel activities from the log. So sequential is appropriate.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: as_loop can exit after first AS, making IQC optional; tst_loop can exit after first TST, making RT optional. Thats correct. In case 4, there was no IQC at all, and no RT. Thats fine.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In the loop definition for POWL, the loop * (A, B) means: do A, then either exit the loop, or execute B and then A again. This matches the observed pattern.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now, double-check the log: In case 3, after AS loop, there is TST, RT, TST. Thats fine. In case 2, no RT, so TST loop exits after first TST. In case 5, multiple RTs. Good.
